Title: Search by full product name (Elastic)
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin > Search
2. Search by full product name
Expected Result: Product found and displayed in results

Title: Search by partial input (few symbols)
Preconditions: Elastic search configured; products indexed including a product named 'Bolt'; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search
2. Select the Products index tab or entity type
3. Enter the first 3 characters 'Bol' in the search field
4. Click Search or press Enter
5. Inspect the returned results list
6. Verify the result count displayed above the results
7. Click on a result to confirm it is a product whose name contains 'Bol'
Expected Result:
- Search field accepts partial text 'Bol' without errors
- Results list is returned within 3 seconds
- Results contain at least one product with name matching 'Bolt' or starting with 'Bol'
- Result count is greater than 0
- No full-name match is required; prefix/partial match is sufficient
- Results do not include products with completely unrelated names

Title: Search by multiple words
Preconditions: Elastic search configured; products indexed including a product described with 'stainless steel bolt'; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search
2. Select the Products index tab or entity type
3. Enter the multi-word query 'stainless steel bolt' in the search field
4. Click Search or press Enter
5. Review the returned results list
6. Verify that results contain products relevant to all or most of the entered words
7. Check that result ranking places the closest matches at the top
Expected Result:
- Search accepts the multi-word input 'stainless steel bolt' without errors
- Results are returned within 3 seconds
- At least one result is relevant to the query (e.g. product name or description contains 'stainless', 'steel', or 'bolt')
- Results are ranked by relevance — products matching more words appear higher
- No error or empty state is shown when multiple words are entered
- Irrelevant products that share no keywords with the query are not returned

Title: Search with incorrect value - no results
Preconditions: Elastic search configured; products indexed; no product exists with the search term used; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search
2. Select the Products index tab or entity type
3. Enter the nonsensical search term 'xyznonexistent12345' in the search field
4. Click Search or press Enter
5. Observe the results area for returned items
6. Check for a zero-results count or empty state message
7. Verify no error page or application crash occurs
Expected Result:
- Search accepts the nonsensical input without errors
- Results list returns zero items
- Result count displays 0 or equivalent (e.g. 'No results found')
- An appropriate empty state message is shown to indicate no matches
- No application error, exception stack trace, or crash is shown
- UI remains fully functional and a new search can be initiated after the empty result

Title: Lucene search by full product name
Steps:
1. Configure Lucene search provider
2. Search by full product name
Expected Result: Product found using Lucene search

Title: Lucene search by partial input
Preconditions: Lucene search provider configured and set as active; products indexed including a product named 'Bolt'; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search
2. Verify that Lucene is the active search provider (check Settings > Search if needed)
3. Select the Products index tab or entity type in the Lucene search view
4. Enter the partial text 'Bol' in the search field
5. Click Search or press Enter
6. Inspect the returned results list
7. Verify result count and relevance of returned products
Expected Result:
- Lucene search provider is confirmed active before executing the search
- Search field accepts partial input 'Bol' without errors
- Results are returned within 3 seconds
- At least one product with name starting with or containing 'Bol' (e.g. 'Bolt') is present in results
- Result count is greater than 0
- Lucene-specific partial text matching is applied (wildcard or prefix matching)
- Results are consistent with what Elastic Search returns for the same partial input

Title: Blue-Green Index Backup Before Rebuild
Preconditions: Blue-green indexing enabled; active index contains indexed data; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search > Index Management
2. Identify the currently active index and note its document count
3. Locate the Backup option in the index management toolbar or context menu
4. Select the active index for backup
5. Click Backup and wait for the backup operation to complete
6. Verify that the backup operation status shows success
7. Initiate a full index rebuild on the active index
8. After rebuild completes, verify the backup still exists and is accessible
Expected Result:
- Active index is identified and its document count is recorded before backup
- Backup option is available in the index management UI
- Backup operation starts without errors
- Backup completion status is shown (success/completed)
- The backup operation does not interrupt the active index availability
- Backup remains intact after a subsequent rebuild operation
- Backup can be referenced for rollback if rebuild fails

Title: Blue-Green Index Swap — Active and Inactive Exchange
Preconditions: Blue-green indexing enabled; both active and inactive indices are built and contain data; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search > Index Management
2. Note the current active index name/identifier and its document count
3. Note the current inactive (standby) index name/identifier and its document count
4. Click the Swap Indexes button
5. Confirm the swap operation in the confirmation dialog if prompted
6. Wait for the swap operation to complete
7. Refresh the Index Management page
8. Verify which index is now marked as active
Expected Result:
- Swap operation is initiated without errors
- Confirmation dialog (if shown) correctly describes the swap action
- After swap, the previously inactive index is now marked as active
- After swap, the previously active index is now marked as inactive (standby)
- Document counts on both indices remain unchanged after the swap
- Swap status is updated in the UI without requiring a full page reload
- System search queries now use the newly activated index

Title: Cancel Ongoing Index Rebuild
Preconditions: Index rebuild is in progress (triggered via Build or Rebuild action); admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search > Index Management
2. Initiate a full index rebuild by clicking Build or Rebuild
3. Observe that the index status changes to 'Building' or 'In Progress'
4. While the build is actively running, locate the Cancel button
5. Click Cancel to stop the ongoing rebuild
6. Wait for the cancellation to be acknowledged by the system
7. Refresh the Index Management blade
8. Verify the final state of the index after cancellation
Expected Result:
- Index build starts successfully and status shows 'Building' or 'In Progress'
- Cancel button is visible and clickable while the build is in progress
- Cancellation request is accepted without errors
- Index status transitions from 'Building' to 'Cancelled' or 'Idle' after cancellation
- System does not crash or enter an inconsistent state after cancellation
- Partial index data from the cancelled build does not corrupt the existing active index
- A new build can be initiated successfully after the cancellation

Title: Multi-Instance Index Refresh
Preconditions: Multi-instance Virto Commerce deployment configured; search index exists; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search > Index Management
2. Verify the current index status and note the document count
3. Add a new product or modify an existing product to create a pending index change
4. Navigate back to Search > Index Management
5. Click the Refresh button on the index blade
6. Observe the index status and document count after refresh
7. Verify that the refresh operation broadcasts to all running instances
8. Confirm that search results on the storefront reflect the refreshed index data
Expected Result:
- Refresh button is available in the index management UI
- Refresh operation completes without errors
- Document count updates after the refresh to reflect the latest indexed data
- Index status shows 'Active' or 'Ready' after successful refresh
- Newly added or modified product is discoverable in search after the refresh
- Multi-instance coordination ensures all instances serve the refreshed index
- No stale data returned from any instance after refresh completes

Title: Blue-Green Multi-Instance Index Coordination
Preconditions: Multi-instance deployment with blue-green indexing enabled; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search > Index Management
2. Note the currently active index identifier across the instance configuration
3. Build a new index on the inactive (standby) slot
4. Wait for the inactive index build to complete successfully
5. Execute a Swap Indexes operation to promote the standby index to active
6. Verify the swap completes in the primary instance
7. Check each additional instance to confirm they all report the same active index
8. Execute a search query from the storefront and inspect which index serves the results
Expected Result:
- All instances initially use the same active index before the swap
- Inactive index build completes without affecting active index service
- Swap operation is reflected across all instances within a reasonable timeframe (e.g., < 30 seconds)
- All instances report the same newly active index after the swap
- No instance continues to serve search results from the old (now inactive) index after the coordination period
- Search queries return consistent results across all instances after swap
- Zero downtime observed during the swap coordination

Title: Partial Index Rebuild — Single Document Type
Steps: Customers
Expected Result: Orders); admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
References:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Search > Index Management
2. Identify available document type options (e.g. Product, Customer, Order, Category)
3. Select only the 'Product' document type for rebuild
4. Click Build or Rebuild for the selected document type only
5. Monitor the rebuild progress and wait for completion
6. Verify that only the Product index was rebuilt by checking index timestamps or build logs
7. Verify that other document type indices (Customer, Order) were not rebuilt
8. Run a product search to confirm products are correctly indexed after partial rebuild
Automation Status:
- Document type selector allows choosing a single type for rebuild
- Partial rebuild starts for the selected document type only
- Rebuild completion status shows success for the Product index
- Index timestamp for Products is updated to the current time
- Index timestamps for other document types (Customer, Order) remain unchanged
- Product search returns correct results after the partial rebuild
- System stability is maintained; no errors or corruption in non-rebuilt indices

Title: Aggregation Properties - Configure Range Facet for Numeric Property
Preconditions: Admin logged into ${BACK_URL}; Store exists with catalog containing products with a numeric property (e.g. Volume_ml); search index active
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Stores > @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id) > Aggregation properties
2. Click 'Add' to create a new aggregation property
3. Select the numeric property (e.g. 'Volume_ml') from the property list
4. Set Aggregation Type to 'Range'
5. Configure range buckets (e.g. 0-100, 100-300, 300-600)
6. Save the aggregation property configuration
7. Navigate to Search > Index Management and rebuild the product index
8. Wait for index rebuild to complete
9. Navigate to the storefront category containing products with Volume_ml values
10. Verify the range filter appears in the sidebar/filter bar with configured buckets
Expected Result:
- Aggregation property selector lists available numeric properties from the catalog
- 'Range' aggregation type is selectable for numeric/money properties
- Range buckets can be configured with from/to values
- Configuration saves without errors
- After index rebuild, the storefront displays the range facet with configured bucket labels
- Bucket product counts are non-zero for ranges containing products
- Clicking a range bucket filters products to those within the specified range
- Property names with underscores (e.g. Volume_ml) are preserved correctly in the UI and filter URL
